initial.prompt:
<context>
You are an expert at solving ARC-AGI (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus) puzzles using Answer Set Programming (ASP).

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>
- `input(row, col, color)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- `color(0..9)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- the output predicate is always `output(row, col, color)` — do not redefine it.
- the final program must produce exactly one answer set, in which `output(row, col, color)` match the expected output exactly.
</rules>

Below are two fully worked examples.

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

% Determine dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Determine reachable zeros from boundary
at_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.

reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), at_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), reachable(NR,NC), delta(NR,DC), input(NR,NC,0), R = NR, C = NC.

% interior zeros: zeros not reachable
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% Output cell exists
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Output: interior zeros become 3, others become 0
1 { output(R,C,3) : interior_zero(R,C), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.
1 { output(R,C,0) : not interior_zero(R,C), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

We need to also ensure that there are no other colors (like 2) present; but those cells will be output 0, matching expected (since expected has no 2). Good.

% Define colors
color(0). color(1). color(2). color(3). color(4). color(5). color(6). color(7). color(8). color(9).

% Define output cell
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one output per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% Compute max row/col
max_row(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.

% At boundary
at_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- max_row(R).
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- max_col(C).

% Reachable zeros from boundary (through zeros only)
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), at_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), reachable(NR,NC), delta(NR,DC), input(NR,NC,0), R = NR, C = NC.

% Interior zeros (zeros not reachable)
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% Constraints to force correct colors
:- interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 3.
:- not interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.

% Colors
color(0). color(1). color(2). color(3). color(4). color(5). color(6). color(7). color(8). color(9).

% Output cell definition
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one output per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% Determine max row and col
max_row(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.

% Boundary predicate
at_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- max_row(R).
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- max_col(C).

% Reachable zeros via 4-neighbor traversal

reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), at_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), reachable(NR,NC), delta(NR,DC), input(NR,NC,0), R = NR, C = NC.

% Interior zeros (zeros not reachable)
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% Force colors: interior zeros become 3, others become 0
:- interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 3.
:- not interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.

```asp
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Colors 0..9
% -------------------------------------------------
color(0). color(1). color(2). color(3). color(4). color(5).
color(6). color(7). color(8). color(9).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output cells – one output colour for each grid position
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions – maximum row / column indices
% -------------------------------------------------
max_row(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Boundary predicate: a cell is on the boundary if it lies
%  on the first/last row or the first/last column
% -------------------------------------------------
at_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- max_row(R).
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- max_col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
% -------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Zeros that can be reached from the boundary
%  (moving only through cells of value 0)
% -------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), at_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  input(R,C,0), R = NR + DR, C = NC + DC.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior zeros – zeros that are NOT reachable
% -------------------------------------------------
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Force colours:
%   – interior zeros become colour 3
%   – every other cell becomes colour 0
% -------------------------------------------------
:- interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 3.
:- not interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.
</asp>
```

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Colors 0..9
% -------------------------------------------------
color(0). color(1). color(2). color(3). color(4). color(5).
color(6). color(7). color(8). color(9).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output cells – one output colour for each grid position
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions – maximum row / column indices
% -------------------------------------------------
max_row(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Boundary predicate: a cell is on the boundary if it lies
%  on the first/last row or the first/last column
% -------------------------------------------------
at_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- max_row(R).
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- max_col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
% -------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Zeros that can be reached from the boundary
%  (moving only through cells of value 0)
% -------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), at_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  input(R,C,0), R = NR + DR, C = NC + DC.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior zeros – zeros that are NOT reachable
% -------------------------------------------------
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Force colours:
%   – interior zeros become colour 3
%   – every other cell becomes colour 0
% -------------------------------------------------
:- interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 3.
:- not interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.

full_program:
% -------------------------------------------------
% Colors (the solver injects these automatically, but we list them for clarity)
color(0).
color(1).
color(2).
color(3).
color(4).
color(5).
color(6).
color(7).
color(8).
color(9).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Grid dimensions (maximum row and column indices)
max_r = #max{Ri : input(Ri, Cj, Col)}.   % highest row index
max_c = #max{Cj : input(Ri, Cj, Col)}.   % highest column index

% -------------------------------------------------
% Wall colour – the (unique) non‑zero colour that forms the enclosing wall
wall = #max{W : input(Ri, Cj, W), W != 0}.   % colour of the wall cells

% Next colour to write into interior holes (wall + 1)
next_colour = wall + 1.

% 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Border cells – zero cells that lie on the outer border
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R = 0.
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R = max_r.
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = 0.
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = max_c.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Flood‑fill reachable zero cells (movement only through cells that are not the wall)
reachable(R,C) :- border(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC,
                  input(R,C,V), V != wall.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Interior zero cells – zeros that cannot be reached from the border
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Output definition
output(R,C,Color) :- interior_zero(R,C), Color = next_colour, color(Color).
output(R,C,Color) :- not interior_zero(R,C), Color = 0, color(Color).

% Exactly one output colour for each input cell
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% -------------------------------------------------
% No cell may have two different colours
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.

You are an expert at solving ARC-AGI (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus) puzzles using Answer Set Programming (ASP).

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>
- `input(row, col, color)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.

- the output predicate is always `output(row, col, color)` — do not redefine it.
- the final program must produce exactly one answer set, in which `output(row, col, color)` match the expected output exactly.
</rules>

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Colors 0..9
% -------------------------------------------------
color(0). color(1). color(2). color(3). color(4). color(5).
color(6). color(7). color(8). color(9).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output cells – one output colour for each grid position
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions – maximum row / column indices
% -------------------------------------------------
max_row(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Boundary predicate: a cell is on the boundary if it lies
%  on the first/last row or the first/last column
% -------------------------------------------------
at_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- max_row(R).
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- max_col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
% -------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Zeros that can be reached from the boundary
%  (moving only through cells of value 0)
% -------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), at_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  input(R,C,0), R = NR + DR, C = NC + DC.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior zeros – zeros that are NOT reachable
% -------------------------------------------------
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Force colours:
%   – interior zeros become colour 3
%   – every other cell becomes colour 0
% -------------------------------------------------
:- interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 3.
:- not interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.
</asp>

max_col(C) :- C = #max{Ci : input(_, Ci, _)}.

boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), (R = 0).

% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R, C) } 1 :- true.

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.

cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

:- cell(R1,C1), cell(R2,C2), R1=R2, C1=C2, output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2), Color1 != Color2.

% Output has the same dimensions as the input.
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R, C) } 1 :- true.

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4‑connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Zeros that can be reached from the boundary
outside(R,C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R,C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R,C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R,C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood‑fill outward through non‑3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color‑3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Zero block: every sub‑cell must be 0
:- input(R, C, 0),
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.

Thus final ASP program:

<asp>
% Colors 0..9
color(0). color(1). color(2). color(3). color(4). color(5). color(6). color(7). color(8). color(9).

% Grid size
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Ri : input(Ri, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Ci : input(_, Ci, _)}.

% Border predicates (separate to avoid unsafe variables)
top(R)   :- R = 0.
bottom(R) :- R = max_row.
left(C)  :- C = 0.
right(C) :- C = max_col.

% Reachable zeros (connected to border through non‑wall cells)
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), top(R).
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), left(C).
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), bottom(R).
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), right(C).

% Flood‑fill through cells that are not walls (value 2)
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC), R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC,
                input(R,C,V), V != 2.

% Interior zeros (zeros that cannot be reached)
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% Determine output for each cell
output(R,C,Color) :-
    interior_zero(R,C), color(Color), Color = 3.

output(R,C,Color) :-
    not interior_zero(R,C), color(Color), Color = 0.

% Exactly one output per cell
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1.

% Uniqueness: at most one output per cell (different colours)
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2), R1=R2, C1=C2, Color1 != Color2.

</asp>

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

% Colors are injected automatically.

% Define wall color (the unique non-zero color). Use #max to get the largest non-zero color.

wall_color(Wall) :- Wall = #max{C : color(C), C != 0}.

% Define next color (Wall+1)
new_color(New) :- Wall = #max{C : color(C), C != 0}, New = #min{C : color(C), C > Wall}.

% Dimensions
max_row(NR) :- NR = #max{Ri : input(Ri, _, _)}.
max_col(NC) :- NC = #max{Ci : input(_, Ci, _)}.

% Boundary predicate
at_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- R = MaxR, max_row(MaxR).
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = MaxC, max_col(MaxC).

% 4-connected offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Reachable zeros from boundary via zeros (non-wall cells)
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), at_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
    R = NR + DR, C = NC + DC,
    input(R,C,Color), Color != Wall.

% Interior zeros: zeros not reachable
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% Hole cells: interior zeros
hole_cell(R,C) :- interior_zero(R,C).
% Neighbor cells that are walls adjacent to interior zeros
neighbor_X(R,C) :- interior_zero(NR,NC), input(NR,NC,Wall), R = NR, C = NC.

% Output definition
output(R,C,Color) :-
    hole_cell(R,C), Color = New.
output(R,C,Color) :-
    neighbor_X(R,C), Color = New.
output(R,C,Color) :-
    not hole_cell(R,C), not neighbor_X(R,C), Color = 0.

% Ensure each cell gets exactly one output color
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% Uniqueness
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.

% define wall color M (max non-zero)
max_color(M) :- M = #max{C : color(C), C != 0}.

% define next color N
next_color(N) :- M = max_color, N = #min{C : color(C), C > M}.

% dimensions
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max{Ri : input(Ri, _, _)}.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max{Ci : input(_, Ci, _)}.

% boundary predicate
at_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- R = MaxR, max_row(MaxR).
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = MaxC, max_col(MaxC).

% flood fill through non-wall cells (i.e., cells with color != M)
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), at_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  R = NR + DR, C = NC + DC,
                  input(R,C,Color), Color != M.

% interior zeros (non-wall zeros not reachable)
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% output for interior zeros become new color N
output(R,C,Color) :-
    interior_zero(R,C), Color = N.

% all other cells become 0
output(R,C,Color) :-
    not interior_zero(R,C), Color = 0.

% each cell gets exactly one output
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% ensure exactly one output per cell
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.

% 4-connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Colors 0..9
% -------------------------------------------------
color(0). color(1). color(2). color(3). color(4). color(5).
color(6). color(7). color(8). color(9).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output cells – one output colour for each grid position
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions – maximum row / column indices
% -------------------------------------------------
max_row(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Boundary predicate: a cell is on the boundary if it lies
%  on the first/last row or the first/last column
% -------------------------------------------------
at_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- max_row(R).
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- max_col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
% -------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Zeros that can be reached from the boundary
%  (moving only through cells of value 0)
% -------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), at_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  input(R,C,0), R = NR + DR, C = NC + DC.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior zeros – zeros that are NOT reachable
% -------------------------------------------------
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Force colours:
%   – interior zeros become colour 3
%   – every other cell becomes colour 0
% -------------------------------------------------
:- interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 3.
:- not interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.
</asp>

<asp>
% ----- Colors -------------------------------------------------
color(0). color(1). color(2). color(3). color(4). color(5).
color(6). color(7). color(8). color(9).

% ----- Grid bounds --------------------------------------------
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Ri : input(Ri, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Ci : input(_, Ci, _)}.

% ----- Border predicates (separate from the main predicate to avoid unsafe variables)
top(R)   :- R = 0.
bottom(R) :- R = max_row.
left(C)  :- C = 0.
right(C) :- C = max_col.

% ----- Reachable cells (zeros that can be reached from the border through non‑wall cells)
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), top(R).
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), left(C).
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), bottom(R).
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), right(C).

% flood‑fill through cells that are not walls (value 2)
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                    R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC,
                    input(R,C,V), V != 2.

% ----- Interior zeros (zeros that are not reachable from the border)
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% ----- Output definition -------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    interior_zero(R,C), color(Color), Color = 3.

output(R,C,Color) :-
    not interior_zero(R,C), color(Color), Color = 0.

% ----- Ensure exactly one output per cell
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).          % each input cell must have an output

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1.

% ----- Uniqueness (at most one colour per cell)
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.

% ----- 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
</asp>

<asp>
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Colors are injected automatically (0..9).
% -----------------------------------------------------------------

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the wall colour – the (unique) non‑zero colour that
%  forms the enclosing walls.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
max_color(W) :- W = #max{C : color(C), C != 0}.

% Determine the colour to write into the holes: the smallest colour
% larger than W.
next_color(N) :- W = max_color, N = #min{C : color(C), C > W}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions and boundary.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{Ri : input(Ri, _, _)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{Ci : input(_, Ci, _)}.

at_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- R = Rmax, max_row(Rmax).
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = Cmax, max_col(Cmax).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill reachable cells (only through cells that are not a wall).
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), at_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  R = NR + DR, C = NC + DC,
                  input(R,C,Color), Color != W.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Interior zeros – zeros that cannot be reached from the boundary.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition:
%   – interior zeros become the next colour,
%   – all other cells become 0.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    interior_zero(R,C), Color = N.

output(R,C,Color) :-
    not interior_zero(R,C), Color = 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Ensure exactly one output colour per cell.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  No cell may have two different colours.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbourhood offsets.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
</asp>

% Grid dimensions
row_boundary(R) :- R = 0.
row_boundary(R) :- R = #max{Ri : input(Ri, _, _) }.

col_boundary(C) :- C = 0.
col_boundary(C) :- C = #max{Ci : input(_, Ci, _) }.

% Flood fill reachable zeros
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, row_boundary(R).
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, col_boundary(C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC), R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC, input(R,C,V), V = 0.

% Interior zeros (zeros not reachable)
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% Output definition
output(R,C,Color) :- interior_zero(R,C), Color = 3.
output(R,C,Color) :- not interior_zero(R,C), Color = 0.

% Ensure each cell has exactly one output
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% Uniqueness (at most one colour per cell)
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.

% Neighbor offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

```asp
% -------------------------------------------------
%  The colour set (0..9) is automatically available.
% -------------------------------------------------

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through zero cells that can be reached from the border.
%  A cell is “border” if it lies on the first/last row or column.
% -------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, R = 0.                         % top row
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, R = #max{Ri : input(Ri,_,_)}.   % bottom row
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, C = 0.                         % left column
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, C = #max{Ci : input(_,Ci,_)}.   % right column

% propagate through zero neighbours
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC,
                  input(R,C,V), V = 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior zeros – zeros that are NOT reachable from the border.
% -------------------------------------------------
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition:
%   – interior zeros become colour 3,
%   – all other cells become colour 0.
% -------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Color) :- interior_zero(R,C),   Color = 3.
output(R,C,Color) :- not interior_zero(R,C), Color = 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one output colour for each input cell.
% -------------------------------------------------
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  No cell may have two different colours.
% -------------------------------------------------
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
% -------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
```

% -------------------------------------------------
%  The colour set (0..9) is automatically available.
% -------------------------------------------------

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through zero cells that can be reached from the border.
%  A cell is “border” if it lies on the first/last row or column.
% -------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, R = 0.                         % top row
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, R = #max{Ri : input(Ri,_,_)}.   % bottom row
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, C = 0.                         % left column
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, C = #max{Ci : input(_,Ci,_)}.   % right column

% propagate through zero neighbours
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC,
                  input(R,C,V), V = 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior zeros – zeros that are NOT reachable from the border.
% -------------------------------------------------
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition:
%   – interior zeros become colour 3,

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  The colour set (0..9) is automatically available.
% -------------------------------------------------

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through zero cells that can be reached from the border.
%  A cell is “border” if it lies on the first/last row or column.
% -------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, R = 0.                         % top row
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, R = #max{Ri : input(Ri,_,_)}.   % bottom row
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, C = 0.                         % left column
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, C = #max{Ci : input(_,Ci,_)}.   % right column

% propagate through zero neighbours
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC,
                  input(R,C,V), V = 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior zeros – zeros that are NOT reachable from the border.
% -------------------------------------------------
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition:
%   – interior zeros become colour 3,
%   – all other cells become colour 0.
% -------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Color) :- interior_zero(R,C),   Color = 3.
output(R,C,Color) :- not interior_zero(R,C), Color = 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one output colour for each input cell.
% -------------------------------------------------
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  No cell may have two different colours.
% -------------------------------------------------
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
% -------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
</asp>

% Colors automatically 0..9

% Grid dimensions and max row/col
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Determine wall color (non-zero color present in input, maximum such)
wall_color(W) :- W = #max{C : color(C), C != 0, input(_, _, C)}.

% Next color (just after wall)
next_color(N) :- N = W + 1, color(N).

% Boundary detection using max row/col
is_on_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0.
is_on_boundary(R,C) :- R = Rmax, max_row(Rmax).
is_on_boundary(R,C) :- C = 0.
is_on_boundary(R,C) :- C = Cmax, max_col(Cmax).

% Flood fill of zeros that can be reached from boundary without crossing wall cells
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), is_on_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC), input(R,C,V), V != W, R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC.

% Zeros that cannot be reached -> interior zeros
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% Output definition
output(R,C,Color) :- interior_zero(R,C), Color = N, color(Color).
output(R,C,Color) :- not interior_zero(R,C), Color = 0, color(Color).

% Exactly one output per cell
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1.

% No two cells have different colors
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.

% 4-connected neighbours
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

<asp>
% Colors 0..9 are provided automatically.

% Determine grid dimensions and max row / column indices
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% The wall colour is the greatest non‑zero colour that actually occurs in the input.
wall_color(W) :- W = #max{C : color(C), C != 0, input(_, _, C)}.

% Any cell on the outer border (first / last row or column) is on the boundary.
is_on_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0.
is_on_boundary(R,C) :- R = Rmax, max_row(Rmax).
is_on_boundary(R,C) :- C = 0.
is_on_boundary(R,C) :- C = Cmax, max_col(Cmax).

% Flood‑fill reachable zero cells (movement only through cells that are not the wall colour).
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), is_on_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC), input(R,C,V), V != W, R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC.

% Zero cells that cannot be reached from the boundary become interior holes.
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% Output: interior holes become the colour just after the wall,
% all other cells become 0.
output(R,C,Color) :- interior_zero(R,C), Color = W + 1, color(Color).
output(R,C,Color) :- not interior_zero(R,C), Color = 0, color(Color).

% Exactly one output colour for each input cell.
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1.

% No cell may have two different colours.
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.

% 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets.
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
</asp>

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Colors 0..9
% -------------------------------------------------
color(0). color(1). color(2). color(3). color(4). color(5).
color(6). color(7). color(8). color(9).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output cells – one output colour for each grid position
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions – maximum row / column indices
% -------------------------------------------------
max_row(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Boundary predicate: a cell is on the boundary if it lies
%  on the first/last row or the first/last column
% -------------------------------------------------
at_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- max_row(R).
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- max_col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
% -------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Zeros that can be reached from the boundary
%  (moving only through cells of value 0)
% -------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), at_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  input(R,C,0), R = NR + DR, C = NC + DC.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior zeros – zeros that are NOT reachable
% -------------------------------------------------
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Force colours:
%   – interior zeros become colour 3
%   – every other cell becomes colour 0
% -------------------------------------------------
:- interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 3.
:- not interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.

% ----- Colors -------------------------------------------------
color(0). color(1). color(2). color(3). color(4). color(5).
color(6). color(7). color(8). color(9).

% ----- Grid bounds --------------------------------------------
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Ri : input(Ri, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Ci : input(_, Ci, _)}.

% ----- Border predicates (separate from the main predicate to avoid unsafe variables)
top(R)   :- R = 0.
bottom(R) :- R = max_row.
left(C)  :- C = 0.
right(C) :- C = max_col.

% ----- Reachable cells (zeros that can be reached from the border through non‑wall cells)
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), top(R).
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), left(C).
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), bottom(R).
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), right(C).

% flood‑fill through cells that are not walls (value 2)
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                    R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC,
                    input(R,C,V), V != 2.

% ----- Interior zeros (zeros that are not reachable from the border)
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% ----- Output definition -------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    interior_zero(R,C), color(Color), Color = 3.

output(R,C,Color) :-
    not interior_zero(R,C), color(Color), Color = 0.

% ----- Ensure exactly one output per cell
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).          % each input cell must have an output

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1.

% ----- Uniqueness (at most one colour per cell)
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.

% ----- 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
</asp>

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Colors are injected automatically (0..9).
% -----------------------------------------------------------------

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the wall colour – the (unique) non‑zero colour that
%  forms the enclosing walls.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
max_color(W) :- W = #max{C : color(C), C != 0}.

% Determine the colour to write into the holes: the smallest colour
% larger than W.
next_color(N) :- W = max_color, N = #min{C : color(C), C > W}.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions and boundary.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{Ri : input(Ri, _, _)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{Ci : input(_, Ci, _)}.

at_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- R = Rmax, max_row(Rmax).
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = Cmax, max_col(Cmax).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill reachable cells (only through cells that are not a wall).
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), at_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  R = NR + DR, C = NC + DC,
                  input(R,C,Color), Color != W.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Interior zeros – zeros that cannot be reached from the boundary.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition:
%   – interior zeros become the next colour,
%   – all other cells become 0.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    interior_zero(R,C), Color = N.

output(R,C,Color) :-
    not interior_zero(R,C), Color = 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Ensure exactly one output colour per cell.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  No cell may have two different colours.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbourhood offsets.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
</asp>

<attempt_4>
<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  The colour set (0..9) is automatically available.
% -------------------------------------------------

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through zero cells that can be reached from the border.
%  A cell is “border” if it lies on the first/last row or column.
% -------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, R = 0.                         % top row
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, R = #max{Ri : input(Ri,_,_)}.   % bottom row
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, C = 0.                         % left column
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, C = #max{Ci : input(_,Ci,_)}.   % right column

% propagate through zero neighbours
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC,
                  input(R,C,V), V = 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior zeros – zeros that are NOT reachable from the border.
% -------------------------------------------------
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output definition:
%   – interior zeros become colour 3,
%   – all other cells become colour 0.
% -------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Color) :- interior_zero(R,C),   Color = 3.
output(R,C,Color) :- not interior_zero(R,C), Color = 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one output colour for each input cell.
% -------------------------------------------------
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  No cell may have two different colours.
% -------------------------------------------------
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
% -------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
</asp>

<asp>
% Colors 0..9 are supplied automatically.
% Determine the grid dimensions (maximum row / column indices).
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Identify the colour that forms the enclosing walls – the highest
% non‑zero colour that actually occurs in the input.
wall_color(W) :- W = #max{C : color(C), C != 0, input(_, _, C)}.

% A cell is on the outer border if it lies on the first or last row
% or on the first or last column.
is_on_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0.
is_on_boundary(R,C) :- R = Rmax, max_row(Rmax).
is_on_boundary(R,C) :- C = 0.
is_on_boundary(R,C) :- C = Cmax, max_col(Cmax).

% Flood‑fill reachable zero cells – movement only through cells that
% are NOT the wall colour.
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), is_on_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC), input(R,C,V), V != W, R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC.

% Zeros that cannot be reached from the boundary become interior holes.
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% Output definition:
%   – interior holes become the colour immediately after the wall,
%   – every other cell becomes 0.
output(R,C,Color) :- interior_zero(R,C), Color = W + 1, color(Color).
output(R,C,Color) :- not interior_zero(R,C), Color = 0, color(Color).

% Exactly one output colour for each input cell.
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1.

% No cell may have two different colours.
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.

% 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets.
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).
</asp>

```
% Colors 0..9 (automatically present)
color(0). color(1). color(2). color(3). color(4). color(5).
color(6). color(7). color(8). color(9).

% Grid dimensions as constants
max_row = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Wall colour: the (unique) non‑zero colour that appears in the input.
wall = #max{C : color(C), C != 0}.

% Offsets for 4‑connected neighbourhood.
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Base reachable zero cells – those on the outer border.
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R = 0.
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R = max_row.
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = 0.
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = max_col.

% Flood‑fill through zero cells that are not the wall colour.
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC,
                  input(R,C,V), V != wall.

% Zeros that are not reachable from the border (interior holes).
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% Output: interior zeros become the colour just above the wall,
% all other cells become 0.
output(R,C,Color) :- interior_zero(R,C), Color = wall + 1.
output(R,C,Color) :- not interior_zero(R,C), Color = 0.

% Each input cell must get exactly one output colour.
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% At most one colour per cell.
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.
```

```
% Colors 0..9 are supplied automatically.

% Grid dimensions as constants
max_row = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Colour of the wall (the unique non‑zero colour that appears)
wall = #max{C : color(C), C != 0}.

% 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets.
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Base reachable zero cells – any zero on the outer border.
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R = 0.
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R = max_row.
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = 0.
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = max_col.

% Flood‑fill: expand through zero cells that are not the wall colour.
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC,
                  input(R,C,V), V != wall.

% Zero cells that are not reachable from the border (holes).
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% Output definition
output(R,C,Color) :- interior_zero(R,C), Color = wall + 1.
output(R,C,Color) :- not interior_zero(R,C), Color = 0.

% Each input cell must have exactly one output colour.
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% Uniqueness of colour per cell.
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.
```

```
% Colors 0..9 are supplied automatically.

% Grid dimensions as constants
max_row = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Colour of the wall (the unique non‑zero colour that appears)
wall = #max{C : color(C), C != 0}.

% 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets.
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Border cells that are zeros (seeds for the flood fill).
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R = 0.
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R = max_row.
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = 0.
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = max_col.

% Flood fill through cells that are not the wall colour.
outside(R,C) :- border(R,C).
outside(R,C) :- outside(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  input(R,C,V), V != wall, R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC.

% Zeros that are not reachable from the border (holes).
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not outside(R,C).

% Output definition:
output(R,C,Color) :- interior_zero(R,C), Color = wall + 1.
output(R,C,Color) :- not interior_zero(R,C), Color = 0.

% Each input cell must have exactly one output colour.
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% No cell may have two different colours.
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.
```

```
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
```

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Colors 0..9
% -------------------------------------------------
color(0). color(1). color(2). color(3). color(4). color(5).
color(6). color(7). color(8). color(9).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Output cells – one output colour for each grid position
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions – maximum row / column indices
% -------------------------------------------------
max_row(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Boundary predicate: a cell is on the boundary if it lies
%  on the first/last row or the first/last column
% -------------------------------------------------
at_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- max_row(R).
at_boundary(R,C) :- C = 0.
at_boundary(R,C) :- max_col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
% -------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Zeros that can be reached from the boundary
%  (moving only through cells of value 0)
% -------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), at_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  input(R,C,0), R = NR + DR, C = NC + DC.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Interior zeros – zeros that are NOT reachable
% -------------------------------------------------
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Force colours:
%   – interior zeros become colour 3
%   – every other cell becomes colour 0
% -------------------------------------------------
:- interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 3.
:- not interior_zero(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.
</asp>

max_col(C) :- C = #max{Ci : input(_, Ci, _)}.

% ----- Border predicates (separate from the main predicate to avoid unsafe variables)
top(R)   :- R = 0.
bottom(R) :- R = max_row.
left(C)  :- C = 0.
right(C) :- C = max_col.

% ----- Reachable cells (zeros that can be reached from the border through non‑wall cells)
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), top(R).
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), left(C).
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), bottom(R).
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), right(C).

% flood‑fill through cells that are not walls (value 2)
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                    R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC,
                    input(R,C,V), V != 2.

% ----- Interior zeros (zeros that are not reachable from the border)
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% ----- Output definition -------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    interior_zero(R,C), color(Color), Color = 3.

output(R,C,Color) :-
    not interior_zero(R,C), color(Color), Color = 0.

% ----- Ensure exactly one output per cell
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).          % each input cell must have an output

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1.

% ----- Uniqueness (at most one colour per cell)
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.

% ----- 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

%  Grid dimensions and boundary.

<asp>
color(0). color(1). color(2). color(3). color(4). color(5). color(6). color(7). color(8). color(9).

% Determine grid size
max_r = #max{Ri : input(Ri, Cj, Col)}.
max_c = #max{Cj : input(Ri, Cj, Col)}.

% Determine wall colour (the non‑zero colour that appears)
wall = #max{W : input(Ri, Cj, W), W != 0}.

% Next colour to use for interior holes
next_colour = wall + 1.

% 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Border zero cells
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R = 0.
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R = max_r.
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = 0.
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = max_c.

% Flood‑fill reachable zero cells (move only through cells that are not the wall)
reachable(R,C) :- border(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC,
                  input(R,C,V), V != wall.

% Interior zero cells – zeros that cannot be reached from the border
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% Output definition
output(R,C,Color) :- interior_zero(R,C), Color = next_colour, color(Color).
output(R,C,Color) :- not interior_zero(R,C), Color = 0, color(Color).

% Exactly one output colour for each input cell
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% At most one colour per cell
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.
</asp>

<asp>
color(0). color(1). color(2). color(3). color(4). color(5). color(6). color(7). color(8). color(9).

% Compute grid dimensions
max_r = #max{Ri : input(Ri, Cj, Col)}.
max_c = #max{Cj : input(Ri, Cj, Col)}.

% Determine wall colour – the largest non‑zero colour that appears in the input
wall = #max{W : input(Ri, Cj, W), W != 0}.

% Next colour for interior holes
next_colour = wall + 1.

% 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Border cells – zero cells on the outer boundary
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R = 0.
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R = max_r.
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = 0.
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = max_c.

% Flood‑fill reachable zero cells (movement only through cells that are not the wall)
reachable(R,C) :- border(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC,
                  input(R,C,V), V != wall.

% Interior zero cells – zeros that cannot be reached from the border
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% Output definition
output(R,C,Color) :- interior_zero(R,C), Color = next_colour, color(Color).
output(R,C,Color) :- not interior_zero(R,C), Color = 0, color(Color).

% Exactly one output colour for each input cell
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% No cell may have two different colours
:- output(R1,C1,Color1), output(R2,C2,Color2),
   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.
</asp>

All variables are ground when they are used, the wall colour is taken from the actual non‑zero values that appear in the input, and the grid size is obtained safely.

max_c = #max{Cj : input(Ri, Cj, Col)}.   % highest column index

% -------------------------------------------------
% Wall colour – the (unique) non‑zero colour that forms the enclosing wall
wall = #max{W : input(Ri, Cj, W), W != 0}.   % colour of the wall cells

% Next colour to write into interior holes (wall + 1)
next_colour = wall + 1.

% 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Border cells – zero cells that lie on the outer border
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), R = 0.

border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = 0.
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), C = max_c.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Flood‑fill reachable zero cells (movement only through cells that are not the wall)
reachable(R,C) :- border(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(NR,NC), delta(DR,DC),
                  R = NR+DR, C = NC+DC,
                  input(R,C,V), V != wall.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Interior zero cells – zeros that cannot be reached from the border
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Output definition
output(R,C,Color) :- interior_zero(R,C), Color = next_colour, color(Color).
output(R,C,Color) :- not interior_zero(R,C), Color = 0, color(Color).

% Exactly one output colour for each input cell
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), cell(R,C) } 1 :- true.

% -------------------------------------------------

   R1 = R2, C1 = C2, Color1 != Color2.
